andrew_encounter.situation: A fisherman is mending nets on the dock. He looks up at you — eyes bright with something restless.

greeting.speaker: Andrew
greeting.text: Hey. You're new around here, aren't you?

greeting.speaker: Andrew
greeting.text: I can always tell. Everyone from Capernaum has that... tired look.

greeting.speaker: Andrew
greeting.text: I used to look like that too. Before I met him.

after_listen.speaker: Andrew
after_listen.text: His name is Jesus. From Nazareth. John the Baptist pointed him out on the Jordan...

after_listen.speaker: Andrew
after_listen.text: He called us 'the Lamb of God.' I didn't understand what that meant. Not then.

after_listen.speaker: Andrew
after_listen.text: So I followed him. Asked him where he was staying. He just said...

after_listen.speaker: Andrew
after_listen.text: And we did. We stayed with him all day. Everything changed.

after_listen.speaker: Andrew
after_listen.text: I found my brother Simon right after. I said, 'We've found the Messiah.' Simon laughed at me. He didn't laugh for long.

after_serve.speaker: Andrew
after_serve.text: You're helping? I... thank you. My hands are giving out.

after_serve.speaker: Andrew
after_serve.text: You know, he asked us to leave all of this behind. The nets. The boat.

after_serve.speaker: Andrew
after_serve.text: My brother Simon thought I'd lost my mind.

after_serve.speaker: Andrew
after_serve.text: Maybe I had. Or maybe I'd finally found it.

after_pray.speaker: Andrew
after_pray.text: Jesus does that too. Sometimes he slips away before any of us wake up. Gone to a quiet place.

after_pray.speaker: Andrew
after_pray.text: I used to think prayer was for the Pharisees. Loud words. Public prayers.

after_pray.speaker: Andrew
after_pray.text: He says the Father sees in secret.

after_pass.speaker: Andrew
after_pass.text: It's alright. Everyone passes by at first.

call_of_peter.situation: Simon Peter is hauling an empty net into the boat. The lines on his face tell the whole story: another night. Nothing.

greeting.speaker: Simon Peter
greeting.text: Don't. Whatever you're about to say, just... don't.

greeting.speaker: Simon Peter
greeting.text: I've been out there all night. Nothing. The lake gives nothing today.

greeting.speaker: Simon Peter
greeting.text: My brother says to come hear this teacher. 'Rabbi,' he calls him. As if I have TIME for rabbis.

jesus_approaches.speaker: Jesus
jesus_approaches.text: Put out into the deep water. Let down your nets for a catch.

jesus_approaches.speaker: Simon Peter
jesus_approaches.text: Master... we worked hard all night and caught nothing.

jesus_approaches.speaker: Simon Peter
jesus_approaches.text: But... because you say so, I will let down the nets.

the_catch.speaker: narrator
the_catch.text: The nets fill. Both boats, full to sinking. Peter falls to his knees.

the_catch.speaker: Simon Peter
the_catch.text: Go away from me, Lord. I am a sinful man.

the_catch.speaker: Jesus
the_catch.text: From now on you will fish for people.

scripture.text: Then Jesus said to Simon, 'Don't be afraid; from now on you will fish for people.' So they pulled their boats up on shore, left everything and followed him.

nicodemus.situation: It is past midnight. An oil lamp flickers on the rooftop. Nicodemus, a member of the Sanhedrin, speaks in a low voice.

greeting.speaker: Nicodemus
greeting.text: I came at night. You understand.

greeting.speaker: Nicodemus
greeting.text: My colleagues on the council... they would not understand.

greeting.speaker: Nicodemus
greeting.text: But I have seen what you do. No one could do these signs unless God were with him.

born_again.speaker: Jesus
born_again.text: Very truly I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again.

born_again.speaker: Nicodemus
born_again.text: How can someone be born when they are old? Surely they cannot enter a second time...

born_again.speaker: Jesus
born_again.text: Very truly I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God unless they are born of water and the Spirit.

born_again.speaker: Jesus
born_again.text: The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you cannot tell where it comes from or where it is going.

born_again.speaker: Jesus
born_again.text: So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.

john_3_16.speaker: Jesus
john_3_16.text: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son...

john_3_16.speaker: Jesus
john_3_16.text: ...that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

john_3_16.speaker: Jesus
john_3_16.text: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.

scripture.text: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.

woman_at_well.situation: Noon. The hottest part of the day. A woman comes to Jacob's Well alone — when no one else would be here.

greeting.speaker: Jesus
greeting.text: Will you give me a drink?

greeting.speaker: Woman
greeting.text: You are a Jew and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink?

greeting.speaker: Jesus
greeting.text: If you knew the gift of God and who it is that asks you for a drink, you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.

greeting.speaker: Woman
greeting.text: Sir, you have nothing to draw with and the well is deep. Where can you get this living water?

living_water.speaker: Jesus
living_water.text: Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again...

living_water.speaker: Jesus
living_water.text: ...but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst.

living_water.speaker: Jesus
living_water.text: Indeed, the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life.

living_water.speaker: Woman
living_water.text: Sir, give me this water so that I won't get thirsty and have to keep coming here to draw water.

you_are_seen.speaker: Jesus
you_are_seen.text: Go, call your husband and come back.

you_are_seen.speaker: Jesus
you_are_seen.text: You are right when you say you have no husband.

you_are_seen.speaker: Jesus
you_are_seen.text: The fact is, you have had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband.

you_are_seen.speaker: Woman
you_are_seen.text: ...Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.

true_worship.speaker: Jesus
true_worship.text: Yet a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in the Spirit and in truth.

true_worship.speaker: Jesus
true_worship.text: For they are the kind of worshipers the Father seeks.

true_worship.speaker: Woman
true_worship.text: I know that Messiah is coming. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.

true_worship.speaker: Jesus
true_worship.text: I, the one speaking to you — I am he.

true_worship.speaker: narrator
true_worship.text: She left her water jar. She went back to the town.

true_worship.speaker: Woman
true_worship.text: Come, see a man who told me everything I ever did.

scripture.text: Jesus answered, 'Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again, but whoever drinks the water I give them will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give them will become in them a spring of water welling up to eternal life.'

sermon_beatitudes.situation: The hillside above the Sea of Galilee. Thousands have gathered. He sits down — which is how teachers teach. He opens his mouth.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.

beatitudes.speaker: Jesus
beatitudes.text: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

salt_light.speaker: Jesus
salt_light.text: You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again?

salt_light.speaker: Jesus
salt_light.text: You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden.

salt_light.speaker: Jesus
salt_light.text: Let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.

scripture.text: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven...
